revision.comment: ページ [[掘立柱]] を [[掘立柱建物]] へ移動: 履歴統合のため
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[掘立柱建物]]

revision.comment: [[掘立柱建物]]2007年12月15日 (土) 13:11版から分割。分割部分の主要執筆者はGreenland4、微修正Kira727、Blueroses5327。
revision.text:
'''堀立柱'''（'''ほったてばしら''' または '''ほりたてばしら'''）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴をつくり<ref>この穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。</ref>、穴の底に立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|堀立柱（高床式倉庫の基礎部分）。[[神奈川県]] [[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]]]
== 堀立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]････ウッド・サークル（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地住居や竪穴住居の主柱･････〈詳細は、''[[竪穴住居]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱･････以下に詳述する。
== 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。
==脚注== <references />
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]]

revision.comment: /* 堀立柱の用途 */ 微調整
revision.text:
'''堀立柱'''（'''ほったてばしら''' または '''ほりたてばしら'''）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴をつくり<ref>この穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。</ref>、穴の底に立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|堀立柱（高床式倉庫の基礎部分）。[[神奈川県]] [[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]]]
== 堀立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]････ウッド・サークル（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地住居や竪穴住居の主柱･････〈詳細は、''[[竪穴住居]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱･････〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉
== 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。

'''掘立柱'''（ほりたてばしら、ほったてばしら）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴をつくりこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、穴の底に立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|堀立柱（高床式倉庫の基礎部分）。[[神奈川県]] [[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]]] == 堀立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地住居や竪穴住居の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[竪穴住居]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

'''掘立柱'''（ほりたてばしら、ほったてばしら）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴をつくりこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、穴の底に立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|堀立柱（高床式倉庫の基礎部分）。[[神奈川県]] [[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]]] == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地住居や竪穴住居の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[竪穴住居]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

'''掘立柱'''（ほりたてばしら、ほったてばしら）とは、土を掘りくぼめて穴をつくりこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、穴の底に立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|堀立柱（高床式倉庫の基礎部分）。[[神奈川県]] [[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]]] == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地住居や竪穴住居の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴住居]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

[[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

revision.comment: Created page with "[[ja:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]][[en:Raised-floor storehouses]]File:Yoshinogari-iseki takayukashiki-souko.JPG|thumb|250px|right|A reconstructed {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|ja|..."

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=Note]]
== References == {{Reflist|2]]
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ishino|first=Hironobu|authorlink=Hironobu Ishino|title=Stories of Ancient Dwellings (Reprint Edition)|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:吉川弘文館]]|series=Historical Culture Selection|year=2006|date=2006-10-20|isbn=4642063021|ref=harv]]
* {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|title=Excavation Survey Manual|chapter=Excavation of Structures|pages=117–224|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:同成社]]|series=Settlement Archaeology Survey, 2nd Edition|year=2013|date=2013-07-26|isbn=9784886215253|ref=harv]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:掘立柱建物]]

In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=Note]]
== References == {{Reflist|2]]
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ishino|first=Hironobu|authorlink=Hironobu Ishino|title=Stories of Ancient Dwellings (Reprint Edition)|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:吉川弘文館]]|series=Historical Culture Selection|year=2006|date=2006-10-20|isbn=4642063021|ref=harv]]
* {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|title=Excavation Survey Manual|chapter=Excavation of Structures|pages=117–224|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:同成社]]|series=Settlement Archaeology Survey, 2nd Edition|year=2013|date=2013-07-26|isbn=9784886215253|ref=harv]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:掘立柱建物]]

[[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
== Notes == {{Reflist|group=Note]]
== References == {{Reflist|2]]
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|last=Ishino|first=Hironobu|authorlink=Hironobu Ishino|title=Stories of Ancient Dwellings (Reprint Edition)|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]]|series=Historical Culture Selection|year=2006|date=2006-10-20|isbn=4642063021|ref=harv]]
* {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|title=Excavation Survey Manual|chapter=Excavation of Structures|pages=117–224|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:同成社]]|series=Settlement Archaeology Survey, 2nd Edition|year=2013|date=2013-07-26|isbn=9784886215253|ref=harv]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]]
{{Archaeology]]
[[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Postholes]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

== References == {{Reflist|2]]

== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

* {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|title=Excavation Survey Manual|chapter=Excavation of Structures|pages=117–224|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:同成社]]|series=Settlement Archaeology Survey, 2nd Edition|year=2013|date=2013-07-26|isbn=9784886215253|ref=harv]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]]
{{Archaeology]]
[[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Postholes]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]

Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

* {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|title=Excavation Survey Manual|chapter=Excavation of Structures|pages=117–224|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:同成社]]|series=Settlement Archaeology Survey, 2nd Edition|year=2013|date=2013-07-26|isbn=9784886215253|ref=harv]]
== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]]
{{Archaeology]]
[[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Postholes]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]

Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

== See Also == * {{ill|Raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] * {{ill|Raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] * {{ill|Pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] * {{ill|Ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]] * {{ill|Posthole buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]]
{{Archaeology]]
[[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Postholes]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==

Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]

[[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]][[Category:redirect]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] cal {{ill|postholes|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:掘立柱]] (support posts), with planks laid over them to form the floor above ground level. In Japanese archaeology, this term distinguishes them from {{ill|pit-houses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:竪穴建物]], which have floors dug below ground level, and from {{ill|ground-level buildings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:平地建物]], where the floor is at or slightly above ground level.
While {{ill|raised-floor warehouses|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] and {{ill|raised-floor dwellings|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床住居]] are well-known examples of ancient Japanese raised-floor structures, other forms like {{ill|watchtowers|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:櫓]] and {{ill|shrines|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:神殿]] are also assumed to have existed. Therefore, the term "raised-floor buildings" is now used comprehensively in archaeology to refer to posthole structures presumed to have been raised-floor constructions.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|author={{ill|Buried Cultural Properties Center|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:埋蔵文化財センター]]|url=https://www.yoshinogari.jp/ym/episode04/house.html|title=1. Housing and Architecture|website={{ill|Yoshinogari Historical Park|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] [[ja:国営吉野ヶ里歴史公園]]|access-date=2022-10-29]]</ref>
Similar to the shift from using "style" (式) in terms like "ground-level style dwellings" to simply "ground-level dwellings," the usage of "style" in "raised-floor style buildings" (高床式建物) has declined.{{Refnest|group=Note|In excavation manuals published by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:文化庁]] since the 2010s, the term "style" has been omitted from these designations.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|pp=117–224]]]]
In actual {{ill|excavation|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:発掘調査]] contexts, most posthole building remains are identified only by posthole traces, with walls, roofs, or above-ground structures rarely preserved. However, the depth of postholes tends to correspond to the height of the original posts, with deeper postholes suggesting raised-floor buildings (like warehouses) and shallower ones indicating lifted-floor or ground-level structures.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]
In post-{{ill|World War II|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] Japanese archaeological history, raised-floor structures were known from {{ill|Yayoi period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:弥生時代]] sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡市]], but were thought absent in the {{ill|Jōmon period|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:縄文時代]]. However, in 1977, posthole alignments suggesting raised-floor buildings were discovered at the {{ill|Agu site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] in {{ill|Hara Village|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:原村]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:長野県]], though initially identified as "rectangular pit groups." In 1988, excavations at the {{ill|Sakuramachi site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] in {{ill|Oyabe City|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:小矢部市]], {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:富山県]], uncovered post materials with mortise holes, confirming that raised-floor buildings existed as early as the Early Jōmon period.{{Sfn|Ishino|2006|pp=187–189]]
== Forms ==
[[File:Ikegami-sone ruins04s3200.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Reconstruction of a raised-floor building in the "roof-storehouse" style at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].]]
Raised-floor buildings are thought to have existed in two forms: one with walls above the floor, and another called "roof-storehouses" (屋根倉, *Yanegura*), where the roof (usually {{ill|gabled|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:切妻屋根]]) rests directly on the floor without walls.{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]] The typical {{ill|raised-floor warehouse|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] is reconstructed with walls, based on findings from sites like the {{ill|Toro site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] and {{ill|Yamaki site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:静岡県]]. Roof-storehouse style reconstructions can be seen in large posthole buildings at the {{ill|Ikegami-Sone site|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] spanning {{ill|Izumi|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:和泉市]] and {{ill|Izumiotsu|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:泉大津市]] in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Postholes-qq]] [[ja:大阪府]].{{Sfn|Agency for Cultural Affairs, Monuments Division|2013|p=165]]

[[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

[[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

[[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==
{{citation needed|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:qq]]
