[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

(Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 6]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン}}). == Footnotes ==
{{citation needed|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Postholes]][[Category:11]] Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} is famous. Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|12=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|12=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|12=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|12=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|12=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|12=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|12=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|12=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=draft}}). == Footnotes ==

({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|12=draft}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|12=draft}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|12=draft}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|12=draft}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|12=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|12=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|12=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|12=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|12=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|12=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|12=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|12=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|12=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|12=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|12=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|12=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|12=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|12=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|12=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|12=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=draft}}). == Footnotes ==

The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==
{{citation needed|ほりたてはしら}} {{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:掘立柱 up to rev 95984024
revision.text:
[[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==
{{デフォルトソート:ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] {{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}}

revision.text:
[[画像:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|復元された[[高床倉庫]]の掘立柱。[[ねずみ返し]]がはめ込まれている。（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[都筑区]]・[[大塚・歳勝土遺跡]]）。]] '''掘立柱'''（ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら）とは地面に穴を掘りこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。、その穴に直接立てた[[柱]]のこと。掘り出した土は柱の周りを固めながら埋め戻していき、建物などの[[基礎]]とする。 == 掘立柱の用途 == # [[祭礼]]でつかう柱や[[ウッド・サークル]]（環状木柱列）を検出した遺跡には、[[富山県]][[小矢部市]]の[[桜町遺跡]]（[[縄文時代]]晩期）、[[石川県]][[金沢市]]の[[チカモリ遺跡]]（縄文後期～晩期）と[[石川県]][[能登町]]の[[真脇遺跡]]（縄文晩期）などがある。伴出の遺物から祭祀や葬送にかかわる施設である可能性が高い。祭礼については後述する。 # 平地建物や竪穴建物の主柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[平地建物]]''・''[[竪穴建物]]'' を参照〉 # 掘立柱建物の側柱 - 〈詳細は、''[[掘立柱建物]]'' を参照〉 == 柱を用いた祭礼 == *日本国内では[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]が著名である。諏訪大社は諏訪湖をはさんで上社と下社に分かれ、さらに上社は前宮と本宮、下社は春宮と秋宮に分かれる。祭礼では、それぞれの社に4本の柱を建てるので、計16本の大木を建てることになる。 *[[柱祭]]は海外にも類似例がある。[[スウェーデン]]の[[夏至祭]]、[[イギリス]]の[[メイポール]]、[[ドイツ]]の[[オクトーバーフェスト]]、[[ネパール]]の[[インドラ・ジャートラ]]、[[インド共和国|インド]]の[[扉曳き祭]]、[[ミャンマー]]の[[柱立て祭]]、[[タイ王国|タイ]]の[[新年の木]]、[[チベット]]の[[聖なる木]]、[[メキシコ]]の[[フライングインディアン]]などである。 ==脚注==
{{デフォルトソート:ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] {{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}}

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==

Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). == Footnotes ==

(Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:考古学|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:建築史|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:建築構造|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} post-built pillars. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:建築物]] [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:遺構|ほりたてはしら]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} post-built pillars. Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is famous. [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezumigaeshi|qq=}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Ward|qq=}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|qq=}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pillars|qq=}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/foundation|qq=}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabe City|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikamori Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazawa City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manawaki Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Post-built Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Post-built Buildings|qq=}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa|qq=}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sweden|qq=}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midsummer Festival|qq=}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/United Kingdom|qq=}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Germany|qq=}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oktoberfest|qq=}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nepal|qq=}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/India|qq=}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|qq=}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thailand|qq=}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tibet|qq=}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mexico|qq=}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]]
