[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Nezumigaeshi|ja|ねずみ返し|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nezumigaeshi|qq=}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}} • {{ill|Yokohama City|ja|横浜市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City|qq=}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki Ward|ja|都筑区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuzuki Ward|qq=}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|ja|大塚・歳勝土遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|qq=}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|pillars|ja|柱|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pillars|qq=}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|foundation|ja|基礎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/foundation|qq=}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Oyabe City|ja|小矢部市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabe City|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Prefecture|qq=}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|ja|チカモリ遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikamori Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Kanazawa City|ja|金沢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazawa City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Manawaki Site|ja|真脇遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manawaki Site|qq=}} in {{ill|Noto Town|ja|能登町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|ja|石川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Prefecture|qq=}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon period|qq=}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Post-built Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post-built Buildings|ja|掘立柱建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Post-built Buildings|qq=}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}} of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa|qq=}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|ja|スウェーデン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sweden|qq=}} ({{ill|Midsummer Festival|ja|夏至祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Midsummer Festival|qq=}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|ja|イギリス|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/United Kingdom|qq=}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Germany|qq=}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|ja|オクトーバーフェスト|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oktoberfest|qq=}}), {{ill|Nepal|ja|ネパール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nepal|qq=}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), {{ill|India|ja|インド|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/India|qq=}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), {{ill|Myanmar|ja|ミャンマー|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myanmar|qq=}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), {{ill|Thailand|ja|タイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thailand|qq=}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), {{ill|Tibet|ja|チベット|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tibet|qq=}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and {{ill|Mexico|ja|メキシコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mexico|qq=}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:History of housing]] [[Category:Types of houses]] [[Category:Raised-floor storehouses forks]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[en:Raised-floor storehouses]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:遺構]]

[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised-floor warehouse|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=}}). == Footnotes ==

(Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Raised-floor storehouses
revision.text:
[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised-floor warehouse|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

(Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "掘立柱" to "掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱"
revision.text:
[[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised-floor warehouse|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

(Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised-floor warehouse|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[sn:Postholes]] [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[sn:ostholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]] [[en:Postholes]]
