revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱]] [[ja:竪穴建物]] [[ja:平地建物]] [[ja:高床倉庫]] [[ja:高床住居]] [[ja:櫓]] [[ja:神殿]] [[ja:文化庁]] [[ja:発掘調査]] [[ja:太平洋戦争]] [[ja:弥生時代]] [[ja:登呂遺跡]] [[ja:静岡市]] [[ja:縄文時代]] [[ja:阿久遺跡]] [[ja:原村]] [[ja:長野県]] [[ja:桜町遺跡]] [[ja:小矢部市]] [[ja:富山県]] [[ja:池上・曽根遺跡]] [[ja:大阪府]] [[ja:切妻屋根]] [[ja:山木遺跡]] [[ja:静岡県]] [[ja:和泉市]] [[ja:泉大津市]] [[ja:吉川弘文館]] [[ja:同成社]] [[ja:掘立柱|1=Postholes|2=ja|3=掘立柱建物]] [[ja:高床建物]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]] [[Category:Foreign language page not connected to wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|1=ja|2=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|qq=|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

[[ja:掘立柱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|1=ja|2=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Postholes]] {{wikidata link|Q11498195}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:掘立柱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

revision.text:
[[ja:掘立柱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|1=ja|2=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architecture]] }} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[en:Postholes]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[en:Postholes]] {{wikidata link|Q11498195}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

revision.text:
[[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:掘立柱]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

A [[:en:Rat guard|Nezumigaeshi]] is fitted. ([[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] • [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama City]] • [[:en:Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|Tsuzuki Ward]] – [[:en:Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins]])]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as [[:en:Column|pillars]] that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the [[:en:Foundation (engineering)|foundation]] of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in [[:en:Oyabe, Toyama|Oyabe City]], [[:en:Toyama Prefecture|Toyama Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), the [[:en:Chikamori Site|Chikamori Site]] in [[:en:Kanazawa|Kanazawa City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late to terminal [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]), and the [[:en:Mawaki Site|Manawaki Site]] in [[:en:Noto, Ishikawa|Noto Town]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] (late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jōmon period]]). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in [[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]] – (see details in ''[[:en:Post in ground|Post-built Buildings]]''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the [[:en:Onbashira|Onbashira Festival]] of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in [[:en:Sweden|Sweden]] ([[:en:Midsummer|Midsummer Festival]]), the [[:en:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]] ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), [[:en:Germany|Germany]] ([[:en:Oktoberfest|Oktoberfest]]), [[:en:Nepal|Nepal]] ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:India|India]] ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Myanmar|Myanmar]] ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tai|Thailand]] ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), [[:en:Tibet|Tibet]] ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and [[:en:Mexico|Mexico]] ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|1=ja|2=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[en:Postholes]] [[en:Postholes]] {{wikidata link|Q11498195}} [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

A {{ill|Rat guard|en|Rat guard|WD=Q11275077|lt=Nezumigaeshi}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} • {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|en|Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153920|lt=Tsuzuki Ward}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|en|Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|WD=Q11433941|lt=Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|Column|en|Column|WD=Q4817|lt=pillars}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|Foundation (engineering)|en|Foundation (engineering)|WD=Q191360|lt=foundation}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in {{ill|Oyabe, Toyama|en|Oyabe, Toyama|WD=Q734366|lt=Oyabe City}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|en|Chikamori Site|WD=Q11317883|lt=Chikamori Site}} in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period}}), and the {{ill|Mawaki Site|en|Mawaki Site|WD=Q11583492|lt=Manawaki Site}} in {{ill|Noto, Ishikawa|en|Noto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349388|lt=Noto Town}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post in ground|en|Post in ground|WD=Q1695743|lt=Post-built Buildings}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post in ground|en|Post in ground|WD=Q1695743|lt=Post-built Buildings}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|en|Sweden|WD=Q34|lt=Sweden}} ({{ill|Midsummer|en|Midsummer|WD=Q154502|lt=Midsummer Festival}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|en|United Kingdom|WD=Q145|lt=United Kingdom}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), {{ill|Germany|en|Germany|WD=Q183|lt=Germany}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|en|Oktoberfest|WD=Q102400|lt=Oktoberfest}}), {{ill|Nepal|en|Nepal|WD=Q837|lt=Nepal}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Myanmar|en|Myanmar|WD=Q836|lt=Myanmar}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tai|en|Tai|WD=Q13367443|lt=Thailand}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tibet|en|Tibet|WD=Q17252|lt=Tibet}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|Mexico|en|Mexico|WD=Q96|lt=Mexico}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==

[[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[File:Takayukasikisouko-nezumigaesi.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Restored {{ill|Raised-floor Warehouse|ja|高床式倉庫|1=Raised floor building|2=ja|3=高床式倉庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor Warehouse|qq=}} post-built pillars. A {{ill|Rat guard|en|Rat guard|WD=Q11275077|lt=Nezumigaeshi|1=Rat guard}} is fitted. ({{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} • {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama City|1=Yokohama}} • {{ill|Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|en|Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153920|lt=Tsuzuki Ward|1=Tsuzuki-ku, Yokohama}} – {{ill|Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|en|Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site|WD=Q11433941|lt=Ōtsuka-Tsugachi Ruins|1=Ōtsuka-Saikachido Site}})]] '''Post-built Pillars''' (ほったてばしら/ほりたてばしら) are defined as {{ill|Column|en|Column|WD=Q4817|lt=pillars|1=Column}} that are erected directly in holes dug into the groundこの穴をホリカタという。「掘り方」「掘方」あるいは「掘形」の字をあてる。. The excavated soil is then re-filled and compacted around the pillar to form the {{ill|Foundation (engineering)|en|Foundation (engineering)|WD=Q191360|lt=foundation|1=Foundation (engineering)}} of a structure. == Uses of Post-built Pillars == # At archaeological sites where pillars used in {{ill|ceremonial rituals|ja|祭礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceremonial rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132241}} or {{ill|Wood Circles|ja|ウッド・サークル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood Circles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (i.e. circular arrays of wooden columns) have been detected, examples include the {{ill|Sakuramachi Site|ja|桜町遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuramachi Site|1=Sakuramachi site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538176}} in {{ill|Oyabe, Toyama|en|Oyabe, Toyama|WD=Q734366|lt=Oyabe City|1=Oyabe, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|WD=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}), the {{ill|Chikamori Site|en|Chikamori Site|WD=Q11317883|lt=Chikamori Site|1=Chikamori Site}} in {{ill|Kanazawa|en|Kanazawa|WD=Q191130|lt=Kanazawa City|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} (late to terminal {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}), and the {{ill|Mawaki Site|en|Mawaki Site|WD=Q11583492|lt=Manawaki Site|1=Mawaki Site}} in {{ill|Noto, Ishikawa|en|Noto, Ishikawa|WD=Q1349388|lt=Noto Town|1=Noto, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} (late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}). Associated artifacts strongly suggest these were facilities related to ritual or funerary practices. (Details of ritual uses are discussed below.) # They serve as the main columns in {{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}} and {{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=Pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Ground-level Buildings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ground-level Buildings|qq=}}'' and ''{{ill|Pit-house Buildings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house Buildings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=Pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q940435}}''). # They are used as the side columns in {{ill|Post in ground|en|Post in ground|WD=Q1695743|lt=Post-built Buildings|1=Post in ground}} – (see details in ''{{ill|Post in ground|en|Post in ground|WD=Q1695743|lt=Post-built Buildings|1=Post in ground}}''). == Ceremonies Using Pillars == * In Japan, the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|WD=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} of {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} is famous. Suwa Taisha is divided by {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} into an Upper Shrine and a Lower Shrine; furthermore, the Upper Shrine is subdivided into a Front Shrine and a Main Shrine, and the Lower Shrine into a Spring Shrine and an Autumn Shrine. During the festival, four pillars are erected at each shrine, resulting in a total of 16 large trees being erected. * Similar {{ill|Pillar Festivals|ja|柱祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also observed overseas, for instance in {{ill|Sweden|en|Sweden|WD=Q34|lt=Sweden|1=Sweden}} ({{ill|Midsummer|en|Midsummer|WD=Q154502|lt=Midsummer Festival|1=Midsummer}}), the {{ill|United Kingdom|en|United Kingdom|WD=Q145|lt=United Kingdom|1=United Kingdom}} ({{ill|Maypole|ja|メイポール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maypole|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨーロッパの五月祭|1=May Day|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q900013}}), {{ill|Germany|en|Germany|WD=Q183|lt=Germany|1=Germany}} ({{ill|Oktoberfest|en|Oktoberfest|WD=Q102400|lt=Oktoberfest|1=Oktoberfest}}), {{ill|Nepal|en|Nepal|WD=Q837|lt=Nepal|1=Nepal}} ({{ill|Indra Jatra|ja|インドラ・ジャートラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Indra Jatra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India|1=India}} ({{ill|Door-pulling Festival|ja|扉曳き祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Door-pulling Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Myanmar|en|Myanmar|WD=Q836|lt=Myanmar|1=Myanmar}} ({{ill|Pillar-raising Festival|ja|柱立て祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pillar-raising Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tai|en|Tai|WD=Q13367443|lt=Thailand|1=Tai}} ({{ill|New Year Tree|ja|新年の木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tibet|en|Tibet|WD=Q17252|lt=Tibet|1=Tibet}} ({{ill|Sacred Tree|ja|聖なる木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Tree|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), and {{ill|Mexico|en|Mexico|WD=Q96|lt=Mexico|1=Mexico}} ({{ill|Flying Indian|ja|フライングインディアン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flying Indian|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Footnotes ==
{{Architecture-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{History-stub|ほりたてはしら}} {{translated page|ja|掘立柱|1=Postholes|1=ja|2=掘立柱|version=95984024|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E9%AB%98%E5%BA%8A%E5%BB%BA%E7%89%A9]] [[ja:掘立柱]] [[en:Postholes]] [[en:Postholes]] {{wikidata link|Q11498195}} [[Category:Archaeological remains]] [[Category:Archaeology]] [[Category:Architectural history]] [[Category:Architecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures]] [[Category:Structural system]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Warehouses]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
